sections.title: Considering Getting a Med Card with NuggMD?

faqs.q: What is a medical cannabis evaluation?
faqs.a: An initial assessment by a qualified physician examining your medical history to determine eligibility. If approved, the practitioner issues an MMJ recommendation—essential for obtaining a med card.

faqs.q: What's the difference between evaluation, recommendation, and med ID card?
faqs.a: An evaluation is the first step. Upon approval, physicians issue a recommendation documenting your qualifying condition. You then register with your state's MMJ program using this recommendation to obtain an official ID card.

faqs.q: Who qualifies for medical cannabis?
faqs.a: Eligibility varies by state regarding age, qualifying conditions, and other factors.

faqs.q: Is NuggMD a medical clinic?
faqs.a: No. NuggMD is a telehealth platform connecting patients with state-licensed evaluating physicians online who conduct assessments and issue recommendations via secure, HIPAA-compliant technology.

faqs.q: Is NuggMD legal and legitimate?
faqs.a: Yes. All practitioners are fully licensed in their respective states with required certifications, operating only in states permitting telemedicine evaluations.

faqs.q: Is NuggMD HIPAA compliant?
faqs.a: Yes. The platform complies with state and federal regulations, keeping patient information confidential with HIPAA-compliant technology.

faqs.q: How does NuggMD protect privacy?
faqs.a: HIPAA compliance protects medical information. Payment processing uses bank-grade encryption. Note: Some states make MMJ patient status available to law enforcement, though not publicly.

faqs.q: Will I be on a public database?
faqs.a: NuggMD never shares patient information without permission. State databases aren't accessible to the general public.

faqs.q: Can I get fired for using medical marijuana?
faqs.a: Protections vary by state. Consult the Learning Center or an attorney for jurisdiction-specific guidance.

faqs.q: Does my med card protect from legal offense?
faqs.a: State and offense-dependent. Cards may provide legal protection or affirmative defense, but not blanket immunity. Consult an attorney for legal questions.

faqs.q: Will other states accept my recommendation?
faqs.a: Each state determines acceptance of out-of-state MMJ cards. Check individual state laws before traveling.

faqs.q: What are NuggMD's hours?
faqs.a: Physicians available 8 AM–10 PM local time daily. Customer support: 5 AM–10 PM weekdays, 10 AM–10 PM weekends. Holiday hours may differ.

faqs.q: How much does an evaluation cost?
faqs.a: With NuggMD, you only pay if you're approved! Pricing varies by state.

faqs.q: Will insurance cover my evaluation?
faqs.a: No. Insurance doesn't currently cover medical cannabis or evaluations, though some states are developing legislation for coverage.

faqs.q: How do I pay?
faqs.a: Major credit/debit cards accepted. HSA/FSA eligible. Itemized receipts provided for HSA/FSA payments.

faqs.q: Are there hidden fees?
faqs.a: No. Pricing is transparent. State registration fees are separate and clearly disclosed.

faqs.q: What's the refund policy?
faqs.a: Cancellations before evaluation incur a $10 processing fee. Most states allow refunds within 30 days if unsatisfied. California permits 14-day refunds if cards haven't been used at dispensaries. Refunds unavailable after practitioner issues recommendations.

sections.title: Preparing for Your MMJ Evaluation

faqs.q: How do evaluations work at NuggMD?
faqs.a: Select your state and click 'Get Your Card.' Answer preliminary health questions, upload photo ID, and provide payment. You'll enter a virtual waiting room where a physician connects with you. Upon approval, receive registration instructions and possibly a PDF recommendation.

faqs.q: When are practitioners available?
faqs.a: Daily 8 AM–10 PM local time. Support chat available 5 AM–10 PM Pacific time.

faqs.q: How do I speak with a practitioner?
faqs.a: After submitting pre-evaluation info and photo ID, you enter the virtual waiting room for face-to-face remote connection. Phone evaluations available in some states.

faqs.q: Are NuggMD practitioners friendly?
faqs.a: Yes, emphasizing patient care and answering questions about conditions and cannabis use.

faqs.q: How long does evaluation take?
faqs.a: A majority of NuggMD evaluations are completed in around 15 minutes, though duration varies by individual needs.

faqs.q: Do I need specific equipment?
faqs.a: Most devices have built-in cameras. Support assists with technical issues.

faqs.q: Can I use phone instead of webcam?
faqs.a: Only in states permitting phone-based evaluations. Contact support for state-specific options.

faqs.q: What about unreliable internet?
faqs.a: Phone evaluations available in some states. Contact support about alternatives.

faqs.q: What do I need for evaluation?
faqs.a: Government-issued photo ID and U.S. phone number. Acceptable ID varies by state.

faqs.q: Do I need medical records?
faqs.a: Some states require only ID and phone number. Others need medical records (prescriptions, letters, x-rays). Recommend having records accessible.

faqs.q: Can NuggMD obtain medical records?
faqs.a: No. This protects patient privacy.

faqs.q: Can I switch providers between evaluations?
faqs.a: Yes. You may use NuggMD after previous providers or services.

faqs.q: Will I get my card after evaluation?
faqs.a: The recommendation is step one. Most states require separate registration before obtaining the card.

faqs.q: Can I use temporary recommendation immediately?
faqs.a: Varies by state. Most require state registration first. Consult your state page or support.

sections.title: After Getting Your NuggMD Evaluation

faqs.q: Can I change my recommendation date?
faqs.a: No. These are legal documents recognized statewide. Only renewal changes dates.

faqs.q: Can NuggMD complete my application?
faqs.a: Most states require individual submission. NuggMD provides step-by-step instructions.

faqs.q: When will I get my card?
faqs.a: Timeline varies by state.

faqs.q: Do I need to renew?
faqs.a: Yes, most states require annual renewal. NuggMD sends email reminders.

faqs.q: Can I renew with NuggMD?
faqs.a: Yes, same process and pricing as initial evaluation, entirely online.

faqs.q: Does renewal cost money?
faqs.a: Most (not all) states charge renewal fees and require re-evaluation annually.

sections.title: After Getting Your Med Card

faqs.q: What benefits does med card provide if recreational is legal?
faqs.a: Potential advantages include: access to additional dispensaries, sales tax exemptions, legal protections (varying by state), parental rights protections, higher possession limits, expanded cultivation rights, earlier purchasing age (18 vs. 21).

faqs.q: Where can I use my recommendation?
faqs.a: Some states accept recommendations; most require official cards for purchases.

faqs.q: How much cannabis can I buy?
faqs.a: Varies by state. Check state websites or Patient Guides for limits.

faqs.q: How many plants can I grow?
faqs.a: Not all states permit cultivation. Those allowing it typically limit patients to six or fewer plants. Review state rules.

faqs.q: Can I share cannabis?
faqs.a: Varies by state with different limits. Check state regulations.

faqs.q: Can I smoke publicly?
faqs.a: Varies from prohibited to allowed in cigarette-smoking areas to lounges. Consult state laws.

faqs.q: How can I verify my card?
faqs.a: Verify online at https://app.nuggmd.com/verify.

sections.title: Considering Getting a Medical Cannabis Card in Minnesota

faqs.q: How does NuggMD work?
faqs.a: NuggMD Minnesota connects prospective patients with state-licensed medical cannabis doctors so you can get your required medical evaluation fast, easy, and online from the comfort and privacy of your own home.

faqs.q: How do you get a Minnesota medical marijuana card?
faqs.a: Qualifying patients must obtain a physician's recommendation and submit information to Minnesota's state registry. Patients receive a registry number rather than a physical card. Initial evaluations cannot be conducted online, but recertifications may be. Approved patients can then purchase products at dispensaries.

faqs.q: Can I get a medical marijuana card without a Minnesota state ID?
faqs.a: Yes, if you lack a government-issued ID showing current Minnesota address, you can provide alternative proof of residency documentation.

faqs.q: How do I find a qualified medical marijuana doctor in Minnesota? Can a dentist or nurse practitioner recommend marijuana?
faqs.a: Licensed physicians, physician assistants, or advanced practice registered nurses can provide evaluations. Dentists cannot recommend cannabis. All NuggMD practitioners are fully-licensed in their respective states.

faqs.q: How much will a NuggMD medical marijuana evaluation cost?
faqs.a: There is no charge if you aren't approved for medical cannabis use.

faqs.q: What are the state fees to get a Minnesota medical marijuana card?
faqs.a: There are no state fees associated with obtaining a medical cannabis card in Minnesota.

faqs.q: Who qualifies for medical marijuana in Minnesota?
faqs.a: Must be a Minnesota resident diagnosed with a qualifying condition by a licensed physician, physician assistant, or advanced practice registered nurse.

faqs.q: What conditions qualify for medical marijuana in Minnesota?
faqs.a: Qualifying conditions include Alzheimer's disease, ALS, autism spectrum disorder, cancer, chronic pain, glaucoma, HIV/AIDS, inflammatory bowel disease, intractable pain, IBS, OCD, obstructive sleep apnea, PTSD, seizures, severe muscle spasms, sickle cell disease, terminal illness, and Tourette syndrome. Patients may also receive certification for any condition approved by their healthcare practitioner.

faqs.q: What if my medical condition isn't listed? Am I still eligible?
faqs.a: Minnesota now allows cannabis to be recommended for any condition that the healthcare practitioner approves.

faqs.q: Can I get a medical marijuana card in Minnesota if I have a criminal record?
faqs.a: Yes, you can become a patient despite having a criminal record. However, certain offenses like drug convictions can prevent becoming a caregiver.

faqs.q: Will health insurance cover the cost of my medical marijuana?
faqs.a: Currently, no. Most insurance companies won't cover cannabis until it achieves federal legalization.

faqs.q: What are the benefits of a medical marijuana card in Minnesota?
faqs.a: Benefits include access to legal, safe, tested cannabis available only through the medical program, and support from a qualified practitioner regarding healthcare questions and medication interactions.

sections.title: Applying for a Minnesota Medical Card

faqs.q: How long does it take to get a medical marijuana card in Minnesota with NuggMD?
faqs.a: Evaluations through NuggMD can be completed in as little as 15 minutes, but the state may take up to 30 days to process applications.

faqs.q: Can I renew my medical card with NuggMD if I was seen by a different doctor last year?
faqs.a: Yes, you have the right to renew with any practitioner. Bring medical records and full history for thorough evaluation.

faqs.q: How much does it cost to renew my medical marijuana card?
faqs.a: Renewal evaluation costs the same as the initial evaluation with NuggMD.

faqs.q: How often do I need to renew my Minnesota certification?
faqs.a: You must renew your certification and state registry every three years.

faqs.q: What if I am not approved for medical marijuana?
faqs.a: You are only charged if approved. If unapproved, your evaluation is free.

faqs.q: What is NuggMD's refund policy? How does the 100% money back guarantee work?
faqs.a: You're charged only if approved. A $10 processing fee applies if you cancel before evaluation. Refunds requested after evaluation but before state submission are available. Once a refund has been initiated, please allow 3-5 business days for the funds to transfer back to the card you used for the transaction. Refunds unavailable more than 30 days after evaluation or after certification submission.

sections.title: How to Use Your Minnesota Medical Card

faqs.q: What types of cannabis products are allowed in Minnesota? Can you buy flower?
faqs.a: Patients may purchase dried cannabis flower, tinctures, topicals, water-soluble cannabinoid products, orally dissolvable products, gummies, and chews. Flower sales are restricted to patients 21 and over.

faqs.q: Where can I buy medical and recreational marijuana in Minnesota?
faqs.a: There are 15 medical marijuana dispensaries in Minnesota. There are two open dispensaries on tribal reservation land from which adult-use consumers can purchase products: Native Care on Red Lake Nation and Waabigwan Mashikiki on White Earth Nation. Additional adult-use dispensaries opened in 2025.

faqs.q: Can I use my Minnesota medical card in other states?
faqs.a: This depends on reciprocity laws in the destination state. Check with your destination state.

faqs.q: How much marijuana can I purchase and possess in Minnesota?
faqs.a: Medical patients are limited to 450 grams of flower per 30 days. Adult-use purchases are limited to two ounces of flower, 8 grams of concentrate, and 800mg of THC in edibles.

faqs.q: How much is the tax on cannabis in Minnesota?
faqs.a: Minnesota doesn't tax medical cannabis products. Adult-use consumers face a 10% cannabis tax, 6.875% state tax, and any applicable local tax.

faqs.q: Can I smoke marijuana in public in Minnesota?
faqs.a: Yes, individuals 21 and over can smoke or vape cannabis where tobacco is allowed. Cities may enact local bans.

faqs.q: Is it legal to drive with cannabis in the car in Minnesota?
faqs.a: No state allows cannabis consumption before or while driving. Transport cannabis home in a sealed container in the glove box or trunk.

faqs.q: Can I get marijuana delivered to my home in Minnesota?
faqs.a: Minnesota doesn't permit medical marijuana home delivery. Patients may use a caregiver's assistance.

faqs.q: Can I grow my own cannabis in Minnesota?
faqs.a: Adults 21 and over can grow up to four mature and four immature plants in an enclosed, locked area away from public view.

faqs.q: How long is a Minnesota medical marijuana card valid?
faqs.a: Your card is valid for three years after the date of issuance.

faqs.q: Can I be evicted for using medical marijuana in Minnesota? Can a landlord refuse to rent?
faqs.a: No school or landlord may refuse to enroll or lease to and may not otherwise penalize a person solely for the person's status as a patient enrolled in the registry program, unless doing so would violate federal law or cause loss of federal benefits.

faqs.q: Can I be fired for using medical marijuana? Can employers drug test for cannabis?
faqs.a: Non-federal employees are protected from discrimination by Minnesota law. Federal workplace protections don't apply.

faqs.q: Can a school or university deny me admission as a medical marijuana patient?
faqs.a: No. Minnesota prohibits schools from refusing enrollment or penalizing individuals solely for registered patient status.

faqs.q: Can I be denied an organ transplant as a medical marijuana patient?
faqs.a: A registry program enrollee's use of medical cannabis is considered the equivalent of the authorized use of any other medication and does not constitute the use of an illicit substance or otherwise disqualify a patient from needed medical care.

faqs.q: Can I own a gun if I have a medical marijuana card in Minnesota?
faqs.a: Federal law prohibits firearm purchases for medical cannabis users, even under state law.

faqs.q: Is it possible for my city or town to ban cannabis facilities?
faqs.a: As of January 1, 2025, cities cannot prohibit cannabis businesses but can limit retailers to one per 12,500 residents.

sections.title: How to Become a Caregiver in Minnesota

faqs.q: How are primary caregivers chosen in Minnesota?
faqs.a: Primary caregivers are designated by the patient during application.

faqs.q: How is the decision made as to whether a medical patient needs a primary caregiver?
faqs.a: Patients are responsible for selecting caregivers and adding their information during enrollment. The state reviews the application. Parents, spouses, and legal guardians aren't required to submit background checks.

faqs.q: How can I change who my primary caregiver is?
faqs.a: Contact the medical marijuana program to change or add a caregiver.

faqs.q: Who can become a primary caregiver in Minnesota?
faqs.a: Caregivers must be at least 18 years old, pass a criminal background check (unless spouse, parent, or guardian), be designated by the patient, and receive state approval.

faqs.q: Can I become a medical marijuana caregiver if I have a criminal record?
faqs.a: Caregivers require background checks unless they are the patient's parent, guardian, or spouse.

faqs.q: Can a minor be a medical marijuana patient in Minnesota?
faqs.a: Yes, minors meeting qualified condition criteria can become patients with a parent or legal guardian serving as caregiver.

faqs.q: Are caregivers allowed to provide care for multiple patients?
faqs.a: Yes. Caregivers can care for up to six patients.

sections.title: Considering Getting a Medical Cannabis Card in Florida

faqs.q: How does NuggMD work?
faqs.a: NuggMD Florida connects prospective patients with state-licensed medical cannabis doctors so you can get your required medical evaluation quickly and easily.

faqs.q: How do I get a medical marijuana card in Florida?
faqs.a: Individuals must obtain a recommendation from a qualified physician, then be entered into the Medical Marijuana Use Registry with a patient ID number. Applications are submitted to the Office of Medical Marijuana Use with proof of residency and a 2x2 photo.

faqs.q: Can I get a med card without a Florida state ID?
faqs.a: Florida requires a valid government-issued Florida ID or driver's license to obtain a medical cannabis card.

faqs.q: If I'm a seasonal resident without a state driver's license, how do I prove residency in Florida?
faqs.a: Seasonal residents can provide a utility bill from the last two months plus one of: voter registration, financial institution mail, property tax bill, government mail, or mortgage/lease agreement.

faqs.q: Can all doctors in Florida write medical marijuana recommendations?
faqs.a: Only doctors licensed by the Office of Medical Marijuana Use are permitted to certify medical marijuana patients.

faqs.q: Can a dentist or nurse practitioner recommend marijuana in Florida?
faqs.a: No. Only doctors can recommend medical marijuana in Florida.

faqs.q: How much does it cost for a medical cannabis recommendation in Florida through NuggMD?
faqs.a: NuggMD Florida offers one of the lowest rates to see a Florida state-licensed physician, and even better value for your renewal.

faqs.q: How much does the state of Florida charge to register for a medical marijuana card?
faqs.a: The fee for a medical marijuana card in Florida is $75. This is separate from the cost of your medical evaluation.

faqs.q: Who is required to have a Medical Marijuana Use Registry ID card in Florida?
faqs.a: Florida ruling 64-4.011 states that all Florida caregiver and patients must possess a Medical Marijuana Use Registry card.

faqs.q: Can I get a medical marijuana card in Florida if I have a criminal record?
faqs.a: Yes. Florida does not prohibit individuals with criminal records from becoming medical marijuana patients.

faqs.q: If I'm on probation, can I still obtain my medical marijuana card in Florida?
faqs.a: You should discuss this with your probation officer before applying, as approval varies by individual officer.

faqs.q: Who qualifies for medical marijuana in Florida?
faqs.a: Florida residents with qualifying medical conditions are eligible to apply for the state's medical marijuana program.

faqs.q: What are the qualifying medical conditions for the medical marijuana program in Florida?
faqs.a: Qualifying conditions include cancer, glaucoma, HIV/AIDS, epilepsy, Crohn's disease, multiple sclerosis, PTSD, Parkinson's disease, ALS, comparable conditions, terminal conditions, and chronic nonmalignant pain from qualifying conditions.

faqs.q: What does Florida mean when they say 'medical conditions of the same kind or class'?
faqs.a: The Florida MMJ constitutional amendment allows its state-licensed weed doctors to certify medical conditions of the same class of the specific qualifying conditions.

faqs.q: What if my medical condition isn't listed? Am I still eligible?
faqs.a: You might be. Florida's OMMU includes 'Medical conditions of the same kind or class as or comparable to the others listed.'

faqs.q: Will health insurance cover the cost of my medical marijuana?
faqs.a: Cannabis, whether medical or recreational, remains federally illegal. This status prohibits health insurance from covering medical cannabis as treatment.

faqs.q: What are the benefits of a medical marijuana card in Florida?
faqs.a: Currently, the only way to access legal cannabis in Florida is by having a medical marijuana card.

sections.title: Applying for a Florida Medical Card

faqs.q: How long does it take to get a medical marijuana card in Florida with NuggMD?
faqs.a: Patients can see a doctor for a medical cannabis evaluation through NuggMD in as little as 15 minutes. Once your application is submitted, the state will notify you of its status between 7 and 21 days.

faqs.q: How long does it take for the state of Florida to process a medical marijuana registration?
faqs.a: Online applications take about 10 business days, while mail submissions take 12-15 business days.

faqs.q: Can I renew my medical marijuana card with NuggMD if I used a different practitioner last year?
faqs.a: Yes. You can choose any state-licensed, qualified practitioner for renewal.

faqs.q: How much does it cost to renew my medical marijuana card in Florida?
faqs.a: NuggMD offers Florida medical card renewal evaluations at a discount, so you save more when you come back to (or switch to) NuggMD Florida.

faqs.q: How often do I need to renew my MMJ card in Florida?
faqs.a: Florida medical marijuana cards are usually valid for one year. The expiration date will be printed right on the ID.

faqs.q: How do I look up the status of my Florida medical marijuana application?
faqs.a: Caregivers and qualified patients can email flmmcp@veritecs.com or call (800) 808-9580.

faqs.q: What if I am not approved for medical marijuana?
faqs.a: With NuggMD, you are ONLY charged for your evaluation if you are approved.

faqs.q: What is NuggMD's refund policy? How does NuggMD's 100% money back guarantee work?
faqs.a: You'll only be charged if you're approved for your medical marijuana certification. If not, your evaluation is free! There is a $10 fee if you cancel before your evaluation. Refunds are available within 30 days of evaluation.

sections.title: How to Use Your Florida Medical Card

faqs.q: What types of cannabis products are available to Florida medical marijuana patients?
faqs.a: In addition to dried flower, concentrates, and topicals, Florida medical marijuana patients may purchase lozenges, gummies, baked goods, chocolate, and drink powders.

faqs.q: Can I smoke medical cannabis in Florida?
faqs.a: Yes, you can legally smoke medical cannabis.

faqs.q: What is the legal age for marijuana purchases in Florida?
faqs.a: Individuals must be 18 to purchase medical cannabis in Florida. Minor patients are not permitted to purchase cannabis and it must be done by their designated caregiver.

faqs.q: Where can I buy medical marijuana in Florida?
faqs.a: Qualified patients can fill orders at licensed medical marijuana treatment centers after their physician inputs the order into the Registry.

faqs.q: When can I get medical marijuana edibles in Florida?
faqs.a: Physicians can add edibles to existing certifications by request without requiring re-certification.

faqs.q: What is the THC limit for edibles in Florida?
faqs.a: Edibles in Florida may not contain more than 200mg of THC per package and no more than 10mg of THC per serving.

faqs.q: How much cannabis can MMJ patients buy in Florida?
faqs.a: Purchasing limits for smokable cannabis flower is 2.5 ounces allotted every 35 days and a possession limit of no more than 4 ounces at any given time.

faqs.q: How much do MMJ patients save on taxes in Florida?
faqs.a: Florida medical marijuana patients do not pay taxes on their medical marijuana purchases.

faqs.q: How much cannabis can an MMJ patient possess in Florida?
faqs.a: For smokable cannabis, patients can purchase 2.5 ounces every 35 days and possess up to 4 ounces. Non-smokable limits are set by physicians.

faqs.q: Is it possible to give marijuana as a gift in Florida?
faqs.a: No, it is illegal in Florida to gift any amount of medical cannabis.

faqs.q: Can I use or consume cannabis products in public places in Florida?
faqs.a: No. Medical marijuana must be consumed in private places in Florida, like your home.

faqs.q: Does Florida allow cannabis consumption lounges?
faqs.a: No. Recreational cannabis is illegal and medical cannabis must be consumed privately.

faqs.q: Is it legal to drive with cannabis in the car in Florida?
faqs.a: Cannabis should be in a sealed container or the vehicle's trunk during transport in Florida.

faqs.q: Can I get medical marijuana delivered to my home in Florida?
faqs.a: Yes. Only state-licensed dispensaries can deliver, and you need a valid medical marijuana ID.

faqs.q: Can I use my Florida med card in other states?
faqs.a: This depends on each state's reciprocity rules. Check with your destination state.

faqs.q: Can out-of-state medical marijuana patients purchase medical marijuana in Florida?
faqs.a: No, Florida does not have reciprocity for out-of-state medical marijuana patients.

faqs.q: How many medical marijuana plants can I cultivate in Florida?
faqs.a: Florida's MMJ legislation does not legalize the cultivation of marijuana for personal use.

faqs.q: How long is a Florida medical marijuana card valid?
faqs.a: Your Florida medical cannabis card expires one year from the date of issuance.

faqs.q: Is it possible for my city or town to ban cannabis facilities in Florida?
faqs.a: Yes. Under Florida law, cities can ban cannabis businesses.

faqs.q: Where can additional information be found regarding medical marijuana in Florida?
faqs.a: Visit the Florida Department of Health's website for more information.

sections.title: How to Become a Caregiver in Florida

faqs.q: Who is eligible to be a medical marijuana primary caregiver in Florida?
faqs.a: Any Florida resident over the age of 21 who agrees in writing to assist in the use of medical cannabis by qualified patients, passes a background check unless the caregiver is a close relative of the patient, and has completed a caregiver certification course is allowed to become a caregiver.

faqs.q: How do I sign up to become an MMJ caregiver in Florida?
faqs.a: You must be over 21 and a permanent resident. The patient's healthcare provider adds you to the patient's profile in the Registry, after which you receive an email to complete your application.

faqs.q: Can minors/children enroll in the Florida medical marijuana program?
faqs.a: Yes, but they must have a parent or guardian acting as a caregiver.

faqs.q: Can I serve as an MMJ caregiver for someone in Florida if I'm also a patient?
faqs.a: Yes, but you must apply separately as both a patient and a caregiver.

sections.title: Considering Getting a Medical Cannabis Card in New Jersey

faqs.q: How does NuggMD work?
faqs.a: NuggMD connects prospective patients with state-licensed medical cannabis doctors to get required medical evaluations online from home, fast and easily.

faqs.q: Do I need to get a medical marijuana card to use marijuana in New Jersey?
faqs.a: Yes, New Jersey requires every patient to have an active license to use medical marijuana legally.

faqs.q: If I am a medicinal marijuana cardholder from another state, am I able to purchase marijuana in the state of New Jersey?
faqs.a: Currently, NJ doesn't allow medicinal marijuana purchases using out-of-state medical cards, though this may change pending a new commission decision.

faqs.q: Can out-of-state medical marijuana patients get a New Jersey medical marijuana card?
faqs.a: Yes, they must meet with a registered NJ healthcare practitioner and provide current state medical marijuana registration to obtain a temporary ID valid for six months (non-renewable).

faqs.q: Can I register with a PO Box in New Jersey or elsewhere?
faqs.a: No, all documents must contain a physical New Jersey address to qualify.

faqs.q: Can I get a med card without a New Jersey state ID?
faqs.a: Yes, if your ID doesn't reflect your current address, provide a current NJ government-issued ID or two proofs of residency from: utility/phone bills (within 90 days), IRS/tax documents, property tax bills, jury summons, federal/state/local government documents, bank statements (past three months), three consecutive pay stubs, or official deeds.

faqs.q: What do I need to prove residency?
faqs.a: NJ accepts recent utility or cell phone bills, plus documents from the IRS or State Tax Office only.

faqs.q: How can I find a New Jersey physician who is legally approved or certified by the MMP?
faqs.a: Speak with your current physician about MMP certification. Find official MMP-certified physicians via the New Jersey government website (Patient tab → 'Find a Doctor') or use NuggMD's website to locate fully licensed NJ doctors.

faqs.q: How much does it cost to get my medical marijuana card in New Jersey?
faqs.a: NuggMD offers competitive rates with no-risk evaluation—patients who don't qualify receive free evaluations. After approval, patients register with the state for free digital cards. Physical cards cost $10.

faqs.q: What does New Jersey charge for a medical marijuana card?
faqs.a: New Jersey no longer charges registration fees. Digital cards are free; physical cards cost $10.

faqs.q: Will health insurance cover the cost of my medical marijuana?
faqs.a: Nearly all insurance companies won't cover medical cannabis because it's federally illegal. A proposed bill to require insurance coverage failed to progress.

faqs.q: Is it possible to have my medical marijuana prescription in New Jersey covered by Medicaid or any other health plans?
faqs.a: Currently, medicinal marijuana is not covered under Medicaid or any insurance/health plans in New Jersey.

faqs.q: Is financial support available in New Jersey if I am unable to afford the state fee?
faqs.a: NJ no longer charges digital card fees. Physical cards cost only $10.

faqs.q: Who qualifies for medical marijuana in New Jersey?
faqs.a: Qualifying NJ residents must be diagnosed with a debilitating medical condition listed by the Cannabis Regulatory Commission, evaluated by a state-licensed physician, be at least 18 years old (or accompanied by parent/guardian), and maintain NJ residency.

faqs.q: What conditions qualify for medical marijuana in New Jersey?
faqs.a: Qualifying conditions include: ALS, anxiety, cancer, chronic pain, dysmenorrhea, glaucoma, IBD/Crohn's disease, intractable skeletal muscular spasticity, migraine, MS, muscular dystrophy, opioid use disorder, HIV/AIDS, PTSD, seizure disorders/epilepsy, terminal illness requiring end-of-life care, and Tourette Syndrome.

faqs.q: What if my medical condition isn't listed? Am I still eligible for medical marijuana in New Jersey?
faqs.a: If your condition isn't listed, petition the NJ Cannabis Regulatory Commission to add it, as they maintain the debilitating conditions list and can expand it.

faqs.q: Does opioid use disorder have any effect on my eligibility for a New Jersey medical cannabis card?
faqs.a: Opioid use disorder qualifies as a debilitating condition as of January 2019, but patients must show proof of medication-assisted therapy (MAT) receipt.

faqs.q: What are the benefits of a medical marijuana card in New Jersey?
faqs.a: Medical cardholders access both medical and recreational dispensaries with priority service; larger medical menus; higher purchase limits (3 ounces vs. 1 ounce for recreational users); and no taxes on medical cannabis, providing savings on high state prices.

sections.title: Applying for a New Jersey Medical Card

faqs.q: How long does it take to get a medical marijuana card in New Jersey with NuggMD?
faqs.a: If approved, physician recommendations are submitted within 24-48 hours. State processing typically takes 7-10 business days, though up to three weeks is possible.

faqs.q: Is it possible to register for the New Jersey Medicinal Marijuana Program on my cell phone?
faqs.a: Yes, mobile registration is available. Contact customer service if experiencing difficulties.

faqs.q: How long does it take for the state of New Jersey to process a medical marijuana registration?
faqs.a: The state must respond within 30 days, reviewing applications in received order. You'll receive email notification with next steps.

faqs.q: Can I renew my NJ med card with NuggMD if I was seen by a different doctor last year?
faqs.a: Yes, you can see any NJ state-licensed physician, physician assistant, or advanced practice nurse participating in the medical cannabis program for renewals. All NuggMD practitioners are state-licensed with required certifications.

faqs.q: How much does it cost to renew my medical marijuana card in New Jersey?

faqs.q: How often do I need to renew my New Jersey certification?

faqs.q: What if I am not approved for medical marijuana?

faqs.q: What is NuggMD's refund policy?

Once certified, refunds aren't available. Email support@nuggmd.com with 'Refund Request' subject; allow 3-5 business days. Refunds unavailable after 30 days post-evaluation.

sections.title: How to Use Your New Jersey Medical Card

faqs.q: What cannabis products can New Jersey medical marijuana patients buy?

faqs.q: Are edibles legal under the New Jersey Medicinal Marijuana Program?
faqs.a: Yes, edibles are legal and available under the Jake Honig Compassionate Use Medical Cannabis Act (A20).

faqs.q: Now that I have my New Jersey medical marijuana card, where can I purchase marijuana?
faqs.a: Purchase at state-registered medical marijuana dispensaries. Find operating alternative treatment centers on the New Jersey government website.

faqs.q: What are the recreational cannabis purchasing limits in New Jersey?
faqs.a: Adults without medical cards can purchase 1 ounce of flower or equivalent in concentrates/edibles.

faqs.q: How much medical marijuana can you purchase at an Alternative Treatment Center?
faqs.a: Medical patients can purchase up to 3 ounces every 30 days.

faqs.q: How much medical marijuana can I possess in New Jersey?
faqs.a: You can possess up to 6 ounces of cannabis flower or equivalent in products at any time.

faqs.q: Is it possible to give marijuana as a gift in New Jersey?
faqs.a: You can gift up to 1 ounce to another adult. Taking payment or giving to anyone under 21 is illegal.

faqs.q: Can I smoke medical cannabis in public in New Jersey?
faqs.a: New Jersey adopted regulations allowing consumption lounges as of January 17, 2024.

faqs.q: Once I qualify for a medical marijuana card and prescription, where am I legally allowed to smoke in New Jersey?
faqs.a: Smoke at home, another private residence, or at a licensed cannabis consumption lounge.

faqs.q: Does the state of New Jersey allow for direct home delivery to patients?
faqs.a: Yes, both medical and recreational consumers can use cannabis delivery services.

faqs.q: With my New Jersey medical marijuana card, can I grow my own plants at home?
faqs.a: No, a medical card doesn't permit home cannabis cultivation.

faqs.q: Can I use my New Jersey med card in other states?
faqs.a: This depends on destination state rules; reciprocity varies. Check your destination state's regulations.

faqs.q: Can I be fired by my New Jersey employer for having a medical cannabis card?
faqs.a: The Compassionate Use Act prohibits employers from adverse actions based on medical cannabis patient status unless they prove use affected performance. Consult HR or legal counsel about employer drug testing policies.

faqs.q: The physician misspelled my name and/or address. How do I edit this information?
faqs.a: Contact the Customer Service Unit at 609-292-4024, Monday-Friday, 8AM-5PM. Profile corrections aren't available after physician data entry.

sections.title: How to Become a Caregiver in New Jersey

faqs.q: Is it possible for someone else to pick up my medical marijuana prescription in New Jersey for me?

faqs.q: How do I register as a medical marijuana caregiver in New Jersey?

faqs.a: No, caregiver card validity ends on the date designated on your license.

faqs.q: I have been fingerprinted before for other reasons, do I need to be fingerprinted again to be a caregiver in New Jersey?

faqs.q: Can out-of-state medical marijuana patients buy medical cannabis in Arizona?

faqs.q: How long does it take to get a medical marijuana card in Arizona with NuggMD?

faqs.q: How long does it take for the state of Arizona to process a medical marijuana registration?

faqs.q: What kinds of cannabis products are available to Arizona patients?

faqs.q: What is the legal age for cannabis purchases in Arizona?

faqs.q: Is it possible to give marijuana as a gift in Arizona?

faqs.q: Where can medical cannabis patients use medical marijuana in Arizona?

faqs.q: Can I use my Arizona med card in other states?

faqs.q: Can I be fired for being a medical marijuana patient in Arizona?

sections.title: How to Become a Caregiver in Arizona

faqs.q: Who is eligible to be a medical marijuana primary caregiver in Arizona?

faqs.q: How can I register as a designated caregiver in Arizona?

faqs.q: Can a minor be a medical marijuana patient in Arizona?

faqs.q: How many patients can an MMJ caregiver have in Arizona?

faqs.q: How much cannabis can medical patients buy in Texas?

faqs.q: When can I get medical marijuana edibles in Texas?

faqs.q: How much is the tax on medical cannabis in Texas?

faqs.q: How long is a Texas medical marijuana card valid?

faqs.q: Is it possible for my city or town to ban cannabis facilities in Texas?

faqs.q: Can I become a medical marijuana caregiver in Texas if I have a criminal record?

faqs.q: Can a minor be a medical marijuana patient in Texas?

faqs.q: Can my medical marijuana be picked up by someone else in Texas?

faqs.a: Qualifying conditions include cancer, chronic renal failure requiring hemodialysis, Crohn's disease, diseases causing nausea/vomiting/wasting/appetite loss/cramping/seizures/muscle spasms/spasticity, epilepsy, glaucoma, hepatitis C with debilitating nausea or intractable pain, HIV, intractable pain, multiple sclerosis, other seizure disorders, spasticity disorders, posttraumatic stress disorder, and traumatic brain injury. Mental health disorders are not recognized as qualifying conditions.

faqs.q: What if my medical condition isn't listed? Am I still eligible for medical marijuana in Washington?
faqs.a: Since 2015, expanding the qualifying conditions list is not permitted and would require legislative change.

faqs.q: What are the benefits of a medical marijuana card in Washington?
faqs.a: Non-registered patients can possess amounts equivalent to recreational users and cultivate up to four plants. Database-registered patients can purchase three times the recreational limit, receive sales tax exemptions, buy high-THC products, cultivate six plants (or up to fifteen with doctor approval), and are exempt from the 37% excise tax on Department of Health-approved products as of May 2024.

sections.title: Applying for a Washington Medical Card

faqs.q: How long does it take to get a medical marijuana card in Washington with NuggMD?
faqs.a: Medical marijuana certification arrives via mail within 7-10 days. Patients then take this to a dispensary to receive their card immediately for $1-$10.

faqs.q: How long does it take for the state of Washington to process a medical marijuana registration?
faqs.a: The state doesn't process registrations. Authorized dispensaries print cards on-site after receiving physician certification.

faqs.q: How often do I need to renew my Washington certification?

faqs.q: How much does it cost to renew my medical marijuana card in Washington?

faqs.q: What if I am not approved for medical marijuana?
faqs.a: You are ONLY charged for your evaluation if you are approved, ensuring cost isn't a barrier to access.

faqs.q: What is NuggMD's refund policy?
faqs.a: Charges only apply if approved. Cancellations before evaluation incur a $10 processing fee. Once certification ships, refunds aren't available. Certifications are valid one year and arrive within 5-10 business days. Free reshipment is available within 30 days; thereafter costs $20 per reshipment.

sections.title: How to Use Your Washington Medical Card

faqs.q: What cannabis products can Washington medical marijuana patients buy?
faqs.a: Washington imposes no product limitations. Patients can purchase flower, vapes, concentrates, edibles, topicals, and more. Database-registered patients can buy Department of Health-approved products exempt from the 37% excise tax.

faqs.q: Where can I purchase medical cannabis with a WA med card?
faqs.a: Purchase from state-authorized medical cannabis dispensaries or join cannabis cultivation collectives where members share only with other collective members.

faqs.q: What are the cannabis purchasing limits in Washington?
faqs.a: Non-registered adults and patients can possess: one ounce useable cannabis, sixteen ounces cannabis-infused oral products, seven grams extract/concentrate for inhalation, seventy-two ounces liquid cannabis product, ten cannabis-infused units for ingestion. They can cultivate four plants and keep six ounces at home. Database-registered patients can possess: three ounces useable cannabis, forty-eight ounces cannabis-infused oral products, twenty-one grams extract/concentrate for inhalation, two hundred sixteen ounces liquid product. They cultivate six plants (eight ounces at home) or up to fifteen plants with doctor approval (sixteen ounces at home).

faqs.q: How much medical marijuana can I possess in Washington?
faqs.a: Possession limits vary by registration status. See purchasing limits section above for specific amounts.

faqs.q: Is it possible to give marijuana as a gift in Washington?
faqs.a: Only licensed retailers can legally provide cannabis. Collective members may share within their group exclusively.

faqs.q: Can I smoke medical cannabis in public in Washington?
faqs.a: No, public cannabis use is illegal.

faqs.q: Does the state of Washington allow for home delivery of cannabis to medical card holders?
faqs.a: No home delivery is permitted for medical or recreational cannabis.

faqs.q: Can I use my Washington med card in other states?
faqs.a: Reciprocity varies by state. Check your destination state's specific rules.

faqs.q: Can I grow my own plants at home if I have a Washington medical marijuana card?
faqs.a: Database-registered patients cultivate six plants (keeping eight ounces) or up to fifteen with doctor authorization (keeping sixteen ounces). Non-registered patients cultivate four plants and possess six ounces. Recreational cultivation is prohibited.

sections.title: How to Become a Caregiver in Washington

faqs.q: Can I get a medical card in Washington if I'm 18 or younger?

faqs.q: How can I get set up with a designated MMJ provider in Washington?
faqs.a: Patients must independently find and agree with a designated provider willing to assist with medical marijuana needs.

faqs.q: Are there differing Washington caregiver registration requirements if you are a legal guardian of qualifying minor patient?

faqs.q: Can I be denied an organ transplant if I become a medical marijuana patient in Michigan?

faqs.q: Can I get a medical marijuana card in Michigan if I have a criminal record?

sections.title: Considering Getting a Medical Cannabis Card in Nevada

faqs.q: How do you obtain a Nevada medical marijuana card?

faqs.q: Can I get a medical marijuana card in Nevada if I'm not a resident of the state?

faqs.q: Can out-of-state medical marijuana patients purchase medical cannabis in Nevada?
faqs.a: Yes, individuals authorized under their state laws are deemed equivalent to Nevada registry cardholders.

faqs.q: Can I still qualify for a Nevada med card if I'm under 21 years old?

faqs.q: How much does it cost to get a medical marijuana card in Nevada with NuggMD?
faqs.a: One low evaluation price with no charge if you don't qualify. State registration fees: $50 for one year, or $100 for two years.

faqs.q: Are there any opportunities to apply for veteran or indigent status?
faqs.a: Unfortunately no, though Nevada remains among more affordable states for medical marijuana registration at $50 per year.

faqs.q: Who qualifies for medical marijuana in Nevada?
faqs.a: Nevada residents with state-issued driver's license or ID, no age restrictions (minors need caregivers), at least one qualifying condition, physician recommendation, completed paperwork, and paid fees.

faqs.q: What are the qualifying conditions for a medical marijuana card in Nevada?
faqs.a: Acquired immune deficiency syndrome, anxiety disorders, autism spectrum disorders, autoimmune diseases, anorexia nervosa, cancer, dependence upon or addiction to opioids, glaucoma, cachexia, muscle spasms including those caused by multiple sclerosis, seizures including those caused by epilepsy, nausea, severe or chronic pain, a medical condition related to the human immunodeficiency virus, a neuropathic condition whether or not such condition causes seizures.

faqs.q: What if my medical condition isn't listed? Am I still eligible for medical marijuana in Nevada?

faqs.q: What are the benefits of getting my Nevada Medical Cannabis Card?
faqs.a: Medical cardholders gain exemption from the 10% retail excise tax, larger possession limits (2.5 ounces flower vs. 1 ounce for recreational; 10,000mg THC in concentrates/edibles vs. 3,500mg for recreational), and approval to cultivate cannabis within 25 miles of dispensaries under specific exemption conditions.

faqs.q: What if I am not approved for medical marijuana?
faqs.a: With NuggMD, you are ONLY charged for your evaluation if you are approved.

faqs.q: How often do I need to renew my Nevada certification?

faqs.q: Can I renew my medical marijuana card with NuggMD if I used a different practitioner last year?

faqs.q: How much does it cost to renew my medical marijuana card in Nevada?

faqs.q: Can I get approved for medical cannabis in Nevada if I have a criminal record?

faqs.q: Is it legal to drive with cannabis in the car in Nevada?

faqs.q: Can you get cannabis delivered in Nevada?

faqs.a: Cards are valid for either 1 or 2 years at your registration choice.

faqs.q: Can I still get fired for using cannabis if I have a Nevada medical card?

faqs.a: Doctor's signed approval is required. Nevada caregivers assign to one patient maximum. Indicate caregiver choice upon state portal application submission.

faqs.q: Who is eligible to be a medical marijuana primary caregiver in Nevada?
faqs.a: Caregivers must be 18 or older, possess valid identification, and remain assigned to no more than one patient.

faqs.q: How many patients can an MMJ caregiver have in Nevada?
faqs.a: Caregivers can only be assigned to one patient in Nevada.

faqs.q: Can a minor be a medical marijuana patient in Nevada?

faqs.q: How long does it take for the state of Illinois to process a medical marijuana registration?

faqs.q: Can I renew my medical marijuana card with NuggMD if I used a different practitioner last year?
faqs.a: Yes, patients can choose any state-licensed, qualified practitioner for renewal. All NuggMD practitioners are fully licensed and certified.

faqs.q: How much does it cost to renew my medical marijuana card in Illinois?
faqs.a: NuggMD offers renewals at the same low rate as initial consultations. Renewals are free if not approved.

faqs.q: How often do I need to renew my Illinois certification?
faqs.a: Renewal periods are one, two, or three years depending on what you selected during application.

faqs.q: What if I am not approved for medical marijuana?
faqs.a: With NuggMD, you are only charged for your evaluation if you are approved.

faqs.q: What is NuggMD's refund policy?
faqs.a: Charges apply only upon approval. A $10 fee applies if canceling before evaluation. Refunds available if requested before state submission. After certification submission, refunds are unavailable. Allow 3-5 business days for processing. Refunds unavailable 30+ days after evaluation.

sections.title: How to Use Your Illinois Medical Card

faqs.q: What kinds of marijuana products are available to Illinois patients?
faqs.a: Available products include flower, capsules, edibles, concentrates, tinctures, and oils.

faqs.q: What do I need to purchase adult-use marijuana from a dispensary in Illinois?
faqs.a: Purchasers must be 21+ and present valid ID, similar to alcohol sales. Out-of-state visitors with valid government ID can also purchase with lower limits.

faqs.q: Can IL medical marijuana patients buy cannabis on the internet?
faqs.a: Yes, patients can order cannabis for in-store pickup online.

faqs.q: What is the legal age for marijuana purchases in Illinois?
faqs.a: Residents must be over 21 to purchase adult-use cannabis.

faqs.q: What are the cannabis purchasing limits in Illinois?
faqs.a: Recreational: up to 30 grams of flower, 5 grams of concentrate, or 500mg THC in infused products. Medical patients: up to 2.5 ounces of flower. Dispensaries must allow medical patients to purchase 2.5 times the adult-use limit. Out-of-state visitors: half the adult-use limit.

faqs.q: What are the possession limits for patients and residents in Illinois?
faqs.a: Recreational: no more than 30 grams of flower, 5 grams of concentrate, or 500mg THC. Medical patients: up to 2.5 ounces.

faqs.q: Is it possible to give marijuana as a gift in Illinois?
faqs.a: Yes, gifts are legal if the recipient is 21+, no money is exchanged, and amounts stay within legal limits. Bartering is prohibited.

faqs.q: Can patients order medical marijuana through the postal service?
faqs.a: No, postal delivery of cannabis is prohibited in Illinois.

faqs.q: Where can Illinois residents purchase marijuana?
faqs.a: Cannabis can only be purchased at licensed dispensaries; individual sales are illegal.

faqs.q: Can IL residents use marijuana in public places?
faqs.a: No, public use is illegal. Front porches are considered public spaces. Cannabis use is permitted only on private property.

faqs.q: Can Illinois residents use marijuana while driving?
faqs.a: No, driving under cannabis influence is illegal and results in DUI charges for any motor vehicle.

faqs.q: Are there marijuana delivery services in Illinois?
faqs.a: No, cannabis deliveries are prohibited. Purchases must occur at licensed dispensaries.

faqs.q: Can I use my Illinois med card in other states?
faqs.a: This depends on each state's reciprocity laws. Check destination state regulations.

faqs.q: Is marijuana cultivation allowed in Illinois?
faqs.a: Only licensed medical patients can legally cultivate, growing up to 5 plants maximum.

faqs.q: How long is an Illinois medical marijuana card valid?
faqs.a: Cards are valid for one, two, or three years depending on the initial fee selected.

faqs.q: Can IL medical patients be fired for using medical marijuana? Can employers drug test patients when they apply for jobs?
faqs.a: Cannabis users 21+ and medical patients cannot be fired solely for use. Employers can restrict use during work hours and in certain positions. Federally-funded employers may conduct drug tests and deny employment.

faqs.q: Can Illinois landlords prevent their tenants from using medical marijuana?
faqs.a: Yes, landlords can set cannabis use rules on their property. Review lease agreements before use.

faqs.q: Can I leave the state with marijuana in my possession?
faqs.a: No, crossing state lines with cannabis violates federal law regardless of amount. Cannabis must be used in Illinois.

faqs.q: How can I get in contact with the Illinois medical cannabis program?
faqs.a: Mail: Illinois Department of Health, 535 W Jefferson St, Springfield, IL 62761. Phone: (217) 782-4977. Email: DPH.medicalcannabis@illinois.gov. Website: dph.illinois.gov.

sections.title: How to Become a Caregiver in Illinois

faqs.q: Who is eligible to be a medical marijuana primary caregiver in Illinois?
faqs.a: Caregivers must be at least 21 years old, Illinois residents, willing to assist with medical cannabis use, and without excluded offense convictions.

faqs.q: How do I register as a medical marijuana caregiver in Illinois?
faqs.a: Patients can include caregiver information during their application, or caregivers can apply separately using the MCPP Caregiver Application to link with an active patient.

faqs.q: Can a minor be a medical marijuana patient in Illinois?
faqs.a: No, caregivers must be at least 21 years old (minors can be patients with parental consent but cannot serve as caregivers).

faqs.q: How many patients can an MMJ caregiver have in Illinois?
faqs.a: Caregivers can have only one patient, though patients may have up to two caregivers.

sections.title: Considering Getting a Medical Cannabis Card in Ohio

faqs.q: How does NuggMD work?

faqs.q: How can I find a state-approved Ohio medical marijuana doctor to perform an evaluation?
faqs.a: Visit NuggMD.com/Ohio to start an online evaluation, or browse the state's digital database of registered physicians at med.ohio.gov.

faqs.q: Are medical marijuana cards required in order to use cannabis in Ohio?
faqs.a: Not anymore. Ohio legalized marijuana on November 7, 2023 (Measure 2), allowing possession of up to 2.5 ounces of flower, cultivation of 6 plants, and recreational dispensary purchases. Medical cards still offer significant benefits.

faqs.q: Must I be an Ohio resident to join the medical program?
faqs.a: Yes, only Ohio residents are allowed.

faqs.q: Can I get a med card without an Ohio state ID?
faqs.a: Yes, as of June 2021. Accepted documents include driver's licenses from any state, birth certificates, naturalization certificates, permanent resident cards, and others. Non-Ohio IDs require two proofs of residency.

faqs.q: How much does a NuggMD medical marijuana evaluation cost in Ohio?
faqs.a: NuggMD offers competitive rates with no payment required if unapproved.

faqs.q: What is the cost of an Ohio medical marijuana certification?
faqs.a: Ohio no longer charges registration fees. Patients pay only for medical evaluations.

faqs.q: Are there any opportunities to apply for veteran or indigent status in Ohio?
faqs.a: Previously available, but program registration now costs one penny for all applicants.

faqs.q: Who qualifies for medical marijuana in Ohio?
faqs.a: Ohio residents 18+ with a qualifying condition and physician certification. Minors need adult caregivers and parental consent.

faqs.q: What are the qualifying conditions for medical marijuana in Ohio?
faqs.a: AIDS, ALS, Alzheimer's disease, cancer, chronic traumatic encephalopathy, Crohn's disease, epilepsy/seizure disorders, fibromyalgia, glaucoma, hepatitis C, inflammatory bowel disease, MS, chronic/intractable pain, Parkinson's disease, HIV+, PTSD, sickle cell anemia, spinal cord disease/injury, Tourette's syndrome, traumatic brain injury, and ulcerative colitis.

faqs.q: What if my medical condition isn't listed? Am I still eligible for medical marijuana in Ohio?
faqs.a: A petition process exists to add unlisted conditions, requiring submission to the Ohio State Medical Board. The process takes approximately one year.

faqs.q: Will health insurance cover my cannabis?
faqs.a: No. Federal illegality prevents insurer reimbursement.

faqs.q: What are the benefits of a medical marijuana card in Ohio?
faqs.a: Benefits include exemption from the 10% recreational cannabis tax, increased legal protections, and medical guidance from physicians.

sections.title: Applying for an Ohio Medical Card

faqs.q: How do I register as a patient in the Ohio medical cannabis program?

faqs.q: How long does it take to get a medical marijuana card in Ohio with NuggMD?
faqs.a: Registration and physician connection takes roughly one hour. State processing takes up to one month. Physician approval uploads within 24-48 hours.

faqs.q: How long are Ohio processing times for medical marijuana applications?
faqs.a: Up to 30 days for the Department of Public Health to process applications, then up to 15 days for ID issuance.

faqs.q: I received my registration email. How long is the link valid?
faqs.a: The registration link remains valid for 90 days.

faqs.q: What if I can't find my Ohio registration email or didn't receive one from the state?
faqs.a: Contact the Medical Marijuana Control Program at 1-833-464-6627 or MMCPRegistry@pharmacy.ohio.gov with full name, date of birth, and preferred email.

faqs.q: Can I renew my med card with NuggMD if I was seen by a different doctor last year?
faqs.a: Yes. Patients may choose any state-licensed, qualified practitioner for renewals.

faqs.q: How much does it cost to renew my medical marijuana card in Ohio?

faqs.q: How often do I need to renew my Ohio certification?

faqs.q: What if I am not approved for medical marijuana?
faqs.a: Unapproved applicants incur no charges under NuggMD's model.

faqs.a: Charges apply only upon approval. Cancellations before evaluation incur a $10 processing fee. Post-evaluation refunds available before state certification, with 3-5 business day processing. Refunds unavailable after 30 days or post-state certification.

faqs.q: Will I be refunded if I submit payment to register with the state and then have my status disapproved?
faqs.a: No. The Ohio board issues no refunds despite NuggMD approval.

faqs.q: Can I get approved for medical cannabis in Ohio if I have a criminal record?
faqs.a: Yes, with one exception: parole or probation conditions may prohibit usage.

sections.title: How to Use Your Ohio Medical Card

faqs.q: What types of cannabis products can I buy in Ohio?
faqs.a: Tinctures, oils, dry flower, edibles, and patches. Smoking is prohibited except vaping.

faqs.q: Where can Ohio patients buy medical marijuana?
faqs.a: Any medical cannabis dispensary. Recreational cannabis is legal but transfers are prohibited.

faqs.q: What types of marijuana consumption methods are allowed in Ohio?
faqs.a: Oils, tinctures, plant material, edibles, lotions, creams, and patches. Vaporization is permitted. Smoking/combustion prohibited. Products cannot appeal to children.

faqs.q: How much cannabis can MMJ patients buy in Ohio?
faqs.a: Up to 45 days' worth at one time.

faqs.q: How much medical marijuana can I possess in Ohio?
faqs.a: A 90-day supply broken into categories: Tier 1 flower (8 oz), Tier 2 flower (5.3 oz), oil (53.1 grams), edibles/tinctures (9.9 grams THC), ointments/creams (26.55 grams THC).

faqs.q: Is it okay to smoke cannabis in public in Ohio?
faqs.a: No. Public consumption is banned.

faqs.q: Is it legal to drive with cannabis in the car in Ohio?
faqs.a: Transport is legal if sealed and locked away from driver access. Vehicle use results in DUI charges.

faqs.q: Does Ohio allow medical marijuana delivery?
faqs.a: Not yet, though changes may follow recreational legalization.

faqs.q: Can I grow my own medical marijuana for personal use in Ohio?
faqs.a: Yes. Adults 21+ can cultivate up to six plants per person, maximum 12 per household.

faqs.q: How many plants can I cultivate/grow in Ohio?
faqs.a: Six plants per adult in an enclosed, locked area inaccessible to children.

faqs.q: How long is my Ohio medical card valid?
faqs.a: One year, expiring on the last day of the issuance month.

faqs.q: Does Ohio have reciprocity laws?
faqs.a: The Board of Pharmacy must attempt reciprocity agreements with comparable states, though none currently exist.

faqs.q: Does Ohio recognize medical marijuana cards from any other state?
faqs.a: No agreements exist, so out-of-state patients cannot legally obtain cannabis in Ohio.

faqs.q: Can my doctor deny an organ transplant if I take medical marijuana?
faqs.a: No. Ohio law prohibits medical care disqualification based on compliant cannabis use.

faqs.q: Can I be fired for using cannabis in Ohio even if I have a medical marijuana card?
faqs.a: Yes. Ohio provides no employment protections for medical cannabis users.

faqs.q: How can I contact the medical marijuana program in Ohio?
faqs.a: Contact the Board of Pharmacy at medicalmarijuana.ohio.gov, 77 S High St Columbus OH 43215, or (614) 466-4143.

sections.title: How to Become a Caregiver in Ohio

faqs.q: How do I register as a medical marijuana caregiver in Ohio?
faqs.a: The patient's physician must authorize and link the caregiver in the state database. Patients may link up to two caregivers; each caregiver serves maximum two patients. State Board approval takes up to seven business days.

faqs.q: Who is eligible to be a medical marijuana primary caregiver in Ohio?
faqs.a: Must be 21+ and an Ohio resident.

faqs.q: How many patients can an MMJ caregiver have in Ohio?

faqs.q: Can a minor be a medical marijuana patient in Ohio?
faqs.a: Yes, with parental/guardian approval.

sections.title: Considering Getting a Medical Cannabis Card in California

faqs.q: How does NuggMD work?

faqs.q: How do I get a California medical marijuana card?
faqs.a: An individual must have a physician's recommendation. Registering for a state MMIC card is optional.

faqs.q: What is required to get a medical marijuana evaluation in California?
faqs.a: You need a California state ID, driver's license, or U.S. passport. Medical records are helpful if available. For MMIC registration, you'll also need a doctor's recommendation, valid government-issued ID, a photo from the county office, and proof of residency.

faqs.q: How do I obtain an MMIC?
faqs.a: The California Department of Public Health administers the voluntary Medical Marijuana Identification Card program for qualified patients and caregivers.

faqs.q: What's the difference between a doctor's recommendation and an MMIC?
faqs.a: A paper recommendation is required to access dispensaries legally. The MMIC is optional but provides convenient proof and exempts holders from state sales tax (8-10%). Without an MMIC, medical patients pay recreational pricing.

faqs.q: What if I don't have a California ID? Can I still get a California MMJ card?
faqs.a: Yes. Any U.S. ID works, including passports, visas with photos, driver's licenses, state IDs, green cards, and employment authorization documents.

faqs.q: How can I find a qualified medical marijuana doctor in California?
faqs.a: NuggMD partners with fully licensed California physicians trained and certified by the state.

faqs.q: How much does it cost to get a medical marijuana evaluation in California using NuggMD?

faqs.q: How much does it cost to register for the MMIC in California?
faqs.a: State registration is optional; fees vary by county.

faqs.q: Is an MMJ recommendation the same thing as California MMIC?
faqs.a: No. A recommendation is the minimum required for dispensary purchases. An MMIC provides additional legal protection, proof of status, and tax exemptions. Medical patients should keep physical copies accessible.

faqs.q: Do I need to register with the MMIC?
faqs.a: No, registration is optional. Without it, you'll pay standard taxes on cannabis purchases.

faqs.q: Who qualifies for medical marijuana in California?

faqs.q: What medical conditions qualify for medicinal cannabis treatments in California?
faqs.a: Listed conditions include AIDS, anorexia, arthritis, cachexia, cancer, chronic pain, glaucoma, migraines, muscle spasms, seizures, and severe nausea. Physicians may also recommend for 'any other chronic or persistent symptom' substantially limiting major life activities or risking serious harm.

faqs.q: What if my medical condition isn't listed? Am I still eligible for medical marijuana in California?
faqs.a: Yes. California law allows physicians to recommend cannabis for 'any other illness for which marijuana provides relief' if documented or serious enough.

faqs.q: Will health insurance cover the cost of my medical marijuana?
faqs.a: Currently, no. Coverage may become available once cannabis research expands and Schedule 1 status changes.

faqs.q: What are the benefits that come with a California MMIC?
faqs.a: Medical patients access more dispensaries, higher-THC restricted products, larger purchase amounts (8 oz vs. 1 oz recreational), exclusive sales, exemption from sales tax, and legal protections including transplant protection.

sections.title: Applying for a California Medical Card

faqs.q: How long does it take for the state of California to process a medical marijuana registration?

faqs.q: Does my medical marijuana authorization need to be renewed in California?

faqs.q: Can I renew my California medical marijuana patient status using NuggMD?

faqs.q: Can I renew my med card with NuggMD if I was seen by a different doctor last year?

faqs.q: How much does it cost to renew my medical marijuana card in California?
faqs.a: Renewal costs match initial evaluation rates.

faqs.q: How often do I need to renew my California certification?
faqs.a: Recommendations and MMICs remain valid for one year from issuance.

faqs.q: Can I change the date my recommendation was issued?
faqs.a: No. Recommendations are legal medical documents signed by licensed doctors and recognized by dispensaries and legal offices. Altering dates is unethical and illegal.

faqs.q: What if I am not approved for medical marijuana?
faqs.a: You're charged only if approved. NuggMD believes evaluation costs shouldn't be barriers to access.

faqs.q: How can California residents receive an MMIC?
faqs.a: Qualifying patients apply with their county health department.

faqs.q: How do I register as a medical marijuana caregiver in California?

faqs.q: Can a minor be a medical marijuana patient in California?
faqs.a: Yes, with physician certification and a primary caregiver designated.

sections.title: Applying for a Georgia Medical Card

faqs.q: How long does it take for the state to process my MMJ application in Georgia?

faqs.q: Is it legal to drive with cannabis in the car in Georgia?

faqs.q: Can I get marijuana delivered to my home in Georgia?

faqs.a: Medical cannabis patients cannot cultivate cannabis in Georgia.

faqs.q: Can I use my Georgia med card in other states?
faqs.a: This depends on destination state reciprocity policies, which vary. Some states prohibit out-of-state cards entirely—verify specific state rules.

faqs.q: Does Georgia have reciprocity laws?
faqs.a: Georgia dispensaries may permit purchases with valid state-issued registration cards allowing equivalent low-THC oil possession if you've been in-state less than 45 days. No formal reciprocity laws exist.

faqs.q: Can I be fired for using medical marijuana? Are employers allowed to drug test for cannabis in Georgia?
faqs.a: Yes, employers are still allowed to test for cannabis in Georgia.

faqs.q: Can I be evicted for using medical marijuana in Georgia? Can a landlord refuse to rent to a medical marijuana patient?
faqs.a: Law doesn't specifically address this, as Georgia's prohibition on home cultivation and smoking makes patient use unlikely to cause landlord conflicts. Consult attorneys regarding specific tenancy concerns.

faqs.q: Can a school or university deny me admission because I'm a medical marijuana patient in Georgia?
faqs.a: Georgia's limited program doesn't address these rights. School admission conflicts seem unlikely given low-THC oil's legal use without smoking or cultivation.

faqs.q: Can I be denied an organ transplant if I become a medical marijuana patient in Georgia?
faqs.a: Yes. Georgia hasn't included any discrimination protections yet for medical cannabis patients awaiting an organ transplant.

faqs.q: Can I own a gun if I have a medical marijuana card in Georgia?
faqs.a: Federal law prohibits gun ownership for firearms registration applicants who use cannabis. Georgia hasn't enacted local protections for medical patients.

sections.title: How to Become a Caregiver in Georgia

faqs.q: Who can become a primary caregiver in Georgia?
faqs.a: Only parents of children or legal guardians of adults or children can become medical marijuana caregivers in Georgia.

faqs.q: How is the decision made as to whether a medical patient needs a primary caregiver in Georgia?
faqs.a: The patient's provider registers a primary caregiver if deemed necessary. Currently, only legal guardians or parents qualify.

faqs.q: How are primary caregivers chosen in Georgia?
faqs.a: Your physician designates your primary caregiver if determined necessary. Only parents of children or legal guardians may serve.

faqs.q: Are caregivers allowed to provide care for multiple patients in Georgia?
faqs.a: Only parents of children or legal guardians of adults or children may become caregivers.

faqs.q: How can I change who my primary caregiver is in Georgia?
faqs.a: Your physician must designate your primary caregiver. Only parents or legal guardians qualify for this role.

sections.title: Considering Getting a Medical Cannabis Card in Iowa

faqs.q: How does NuggMD work?
faqs.a: NuggMD connects prospective patients with state-licensed medical cannabis doctors for required evaluations conducted online from home.

faqs.q: Who qualifies for medical marijuana in Iowa?
faqs.a: Any Iowa resident with a debilitating medical condition may apply. Minors require parental/guardian application. All patients need state-licensed doctor approval and state application before using medical cannabis.

faqs.q: What conditions qualify for medical marijuana in Iowa?
faqs.a: Qualifying conditions include: AIDS/HIV, ALS, cancer-related symptoms (pain, nausea, cachexia), chronic pain, corticobasal degeneration, Crohn's disease, MS with muscle spasms, Parkinson's disease, PTSD, seizures/epilepsy, severe autism with behavioral issues, ulcerative colitis, and terminal illnesses with severe symptoms.

faqs.q: What are the benefits of a medical marijuana card in Iowa?
faqs.a: Currently, a medical marijuana card is the only legal way to use cannabis in the state.

faqs.q: How much does it cost to get my medical marijuana card in Iowa?
faqs.a: NuggMD offers low-cost, no-risk evaluations with no charge if you don't qualify. Approved patients pay a $100 state application fee.

faqs.q: What is the cost to renew a medical marijuana card in Iowa?
faqs.a: The state renewal fee is $100, plus NuggMD's renewal evaluation cost at their standard rate.

faqs.q: Will health insurance cover the cost of my medical marijuana?
faqs.a: No, because cannabis remains federally illegal. Insurance coverage may change as states advance supporting measures.

faqs.q: How long is an Iowa medical marijuana card valid?
faqs.a: Cards are valid for 12 months from issuance.

sections.title: Applying for an Iowa Medical Card

faqs.q: How do I get a medical marijuana card in Iowa?
faqs.a: Obtain a recommendation from an eligible practitioner, complete required forms, then apply online through the Iowa Office of Medical Cannabidiol.

faqs.q: What if my medical condition isn't listed? Am I still eligible for medical marijuana in Iowa?
faqs.a: No, but Iowa residents can petition the Medical Cannabidiol Board to add conditions to the qualifying list.

faqs.q: Can I get a med card without an Iowa state ID?
faqs.a: No. An Iowa-issued ID or driver's license is required, submitted as a photo or photocopy.

faqs.q: Can a dentist or nurse practitioner recommend marijuana in Iowa?
faqs.a: Only physicians (MD/DO), physician assistants, advanced registered nurse practitioners, and podiatrists can recommend cannabis. Dentists cannot.

faqs.q: How long does it take to get a medical marijuana card in Iowa with NuggMD?
faqs.a: PDF certifications arrive within 24-48 hours, sometimes same-day. State approval takes up to two weeks; permanent cards arrive via email upon approval.

faqs.q: Can I renew my med card with NuggMD if I was seen by a different doctor last year?
faqs.a: Yes. You can see any Iowa state-licensed MD or DO. All NuggMD physicians are state-licensed with required cannabis training.

faqs.q: What if I am not approved for medical marijuana?
faqs.a: You are only charged for your evaluation if you are approved.

faqs.q: What if I can't find my Iowa med card or didn't receive one?
faqs.a: Contact the Iowa Office of Medical Cannabis at (515) 281-7689 or through their website.

faqs.q: What is NuggMD's refund policy? How does NuggMD's 100% money back guarantee work?
faqs.a: You're charged only if approved. If you cancel before evaluation, a $10 processing fee applies. Once certified, refunds aren't available. Patients not completing certification won't receive refunds. Refunds take 3-5 business days.

sections.title: How to Use Your Iowa Med Card

faqs.q: What types of cannabis products are allowed in Iowa?
faqs.a: Allowed products include tablets, capsules, liquids, tinctures, sublingual, topical gels/lotions, transdermal patches, nebulizers, vaporizers, and suppositories. Smoking flower/joints/concentrates and edibles are prohibited.

faqs.q: Can I smoke medical marijuana in Iowa?
faqs.a: No, but vaporizers are permitted.

faqs.q: What are the allowed hours for marijuana dispensaries in Iowa?
faqs.a: Dispensary hours vary, but none operate before 10 AM or after 7 PM currently.

faqs.q: Where can I buy medical marijuana in Iowa?
faqs.a: Purchase from any of Iowa's licensed dispensaries.

faqs.q: How much cannabis can MMJ patients buy in Iowa?
faqs.a: Patients can purchase up to 4.5 grams of THC per 90 days. Waivers allowing higher limits may be available after initial treatment attempts, or immediately for certain terminal illnesses.

faqs.q: How much cannabis can an MMJ patient possess in Iowa?
faqs.a: Patients may possess a 90-day supply as recommended by their physician.

faqs.q: Can I get medical marijuana delivered to my home in Iowa?
faqs.a: No established delivery programs exist currently.

faqs.q: Where can I buy recreational cannabis in Iowa?
faqs.a: Recreational cannabis isn't legal in Iowa yet.

faqs.q: Can I use or consume cannabis products in public places in Iowa?
faqs.a: No. Cannabis use is restricted to private property like your home.

faqs.q: Does Iowa allow cannabis consumption lounges?
faqs.a: No.

faqs.q: Is it legal to drive with cannabis in the car in Iowa?
faqs.a: You may transport purchased cannabis home, but using it while driving is illegal. Cannabis-impaired driving results in DUI charges.

faqs.q: What are the rules for home cultivation of cannabis in Iowa?
faqs.a: Iowa prohibits home cannabis cultivation.

faqs.q: Does Iowa have reciprocity laws?
faqs.a: Iowa allows out-of-state patients to use allowable medical cannabis products in-state but has no reciprocity laws. Usage rights for Iowa patients in other states depend on those states' policies.

faqs.q: Can out-of-state patients buy medical marijuana in Iowa?
faqs.a: Out-of-state patients cannot purchase from Iowa dispensaries but may possess and use cannabis.

faqs.q: Can I use my Iowa med card in other states?
faqs.a: This depends on destination state reciprocity laws. Each state has different rules.

faqs.q: Is it possible for my city or town to ban cannabis facilities in Iowa?
faqs.a: The issue isn't municipal bans but insufficient state-licensed producers. Legislative efforts are underway to address this.

sections.title: How to Become a Caregiver in Iowa

faqs.q: How do I sign-up to become an MMJ caregiver in Iowa?
faqs.a: Find signup instructions on the Iowa Department of Health Patient and Caregiver information page.

faqs.q: Who is eligible to become a caregiver in Iowa?
faqs.a: Caregivers must be Iowa or bordering state residents (Illinois, Minnesota, Missouri, Nebraska, South Dakota, Wisconsin), at least 18 years old, and designated by a patient's healthcare practitioner as a necessary caretaker.

faqs.q: Can I become a medical marijuana caregiver in Iowa if I have a criminal record?
faqs.a: State regulations don't restrict caregivers with criminal records.

sections.title: Considering Getting a Medical Cannabis Card in Missouri

faqs.q: Who qualifies for medical marijuana in Missouri?

faqs.q: What are the qualifying conditions for medical marijuana in Missouri?

faqs.q: What if my medical condition isn't listed? Am I still eligible for medical marijuana in Missouri?

faqs.q: Can I get a med card without a Missouri state ID?
faqs.a: Yes. Proof of residency is no longer required.

faqs.q: Do I have to live in Missouri to enroll in the state MMJ program?

faqs.q: How do I find a doctor that will talk to me about medical marijuana?

faqs.q: Can a dentist or nurse practitioner recommend marijuana in Missouri?

faqs.q: How much does the state Missouri Patient ID Card cost?

faqs.q: Will health insurance cover my cannabis?
faqs.a: No. Medical marijuana remains federally illegal, so insurance doesn't cover it (except Epidiolex for seizures).

sections.title: Applying for a Missouri Medical Card

faqs.q: How long does it take to get a medical marijuana card in Missouri with NuggMD?

faqs.q: How long does the state have to process my medical marijuana card in Missouri?

faqs.q: How long will it take for my state Missouri MMJ Identification Card to arrive in the mail?

faqs.q: Can I renew my med card with NuggMD if I was seen by a different doctor last year?

sections.title: How to Use Your Missouri Med Card

faqs.q: What types of cannabis products are available in Missouri?
